title: Building Your Money Machine
author: Michael Yardney
subline: How to get your money to work harder than you did for it.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A simple machine: gear train. 3 gears (small, medium, large) connected by hairlines. The largest gear is in RED. The gears spin. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1100)" fill="none">
    <!-- Small gear (left) -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2">
      <circle cx="-400" cy="-100" r="80"/>
      <!-- 12 teeth as small lines -->
      <g stroke-linecap="square">
        <line x1="-400" y1="-200" x2="-400" y2="-220"/>
        <line x1="-321" y1="-179" x2="-307" y2="-194"/>
        <line x1="-300" y1="-100" x2="-280" y2="-100"/>
        <line x1="-321" y1="-21" x2="-307" y2="-6"/>
        <line x1="-400" y1="0" x2="-400" y2="20"/>
        <line x1="-479" y1="-21" x2="-493" y2="-6"/>
        <line x1="-500" y1="-100" x2="-520" y2="-100"/>
        <line x1="-479" y1="-179" x2="-493" y2="-194"/>
      </g>
    </g>
    <!-- Medium gear (middle) -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2">
      <circle cx="-160" cy="80" r="120"/>
      <g stroke-linecap="square">
        <line x1="-160" y1="-60" x2="-160" y2="-90"/>
        <line x1="-75" y1="-27" x2="-50" y2="-46"/>
        <line x1="-40" y1="80" x2="-10" y2="80"/>
        <line x1="-75" y1="187" x2="-50" y2="206"/>
        <line x1="-160" y1="220" x2="-160" y2="250"/>
        <line x1="-245" y1="187" x2="-270" y2="206"/>
        <line x1="-280" y1="80" x2="-310" y2="80"/>
        <line x1="-245" y1="-27" x2="-270" y2="-46"/>
      </g>
    </g>
    <!-- Big RED gear (right) -->
    <g stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2">
      <circle cx="200" cy="-40" r="180"/>
      <g stroke-linecap="square">
        <line x1="200" y1="-240" x2="200" y2="-280"/>
        <line x1="327" y1="-193" x2="356" y2="-220"/>
        <line x1="380" y1="-40" x2="420" y2="-40"/>
        <line x1="327" y1="113" x2="356" y2="140"/>
        <line x1="200" y1="160" x2="200" y2="200"/>
        <line x1="73" y1="113" x2="44" y2="140"/>
        <line x1="20" y1="-40" x2="-20" y2="-40"/>
        <line x1="73" y1="-193" x2="44" y2="-220"/>
      </g>
      <!-- Spokes inside -->
      <line x1="200" y1="-40" x2="80" y2="-200" stroke-width="1"/>
      <line x1="200" y1="-40" x2="320" y2="-200" stroke-width="1"/>
      <line x1="200" y1="-40" x2="80" y2="120" stroke-width="1"/>
      <line x1="200" y1="-40" x2="320" y2="120" stroke-width="1"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The red gear center: a small white dot -->
    <circle cx="200" cy="-40" r="14" fill="#ffffff"/>
  </g>
  <text x="800" y="380" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">GEAR TRAIN · 1:2:4 RATIO</text>
</svg>
rationale: A gear train: 3 gears of increasing size, the largest in RED — the only chromatic element, the gear that does the work. 280px mixed-case 'Building Your / Money Machine' title sits at the bottom in weight 300. The visual thesis: a money machine is literally a machine — a gear train that multiplies effort, and the biggest gear is the one that earns.
